Original post text: this one open source browser just replaced so many tools at once - ChatGPT Atlas - Perplexity Comet - your paid AI browser subscription - Chrome DevTools MCP it's called BrowserOS, a Chromium fork you can connect to any AI agent you like as an MCP server, fully local.
Reply text: fully local MCP is compelling. replacing Chrome DevTools still depends on how well the Chromium fork stays in sync.

Original post text: Agent mentions in Slack and GitHub that auto-route to Codex or Claude Code, returning results in-thread. OpenTag is the open-source tool doing this — 1.3k stars. Worth wiring into your dev workflow now. #AIWorkflow #DevTools
Reply text: the in-thread result is the useful bit. switching agents without leaving Slack or GitHub keeps the loop tight.

Original post text: The rate-limit counter is tiny. The problem is that every request pauses for it. - A database can hold a count - but it is a bad place to put a read/write decision on every request. - Redis fits because this state is hot and usually short-lived. - For a fixed window, the
Reply text: Redis is the easy choice. fail-open vs fail-closed is the decision that bites in production.

Original post text: AI workflows get way more useful when they stop acting like every task starts from a blank page. Most real work already has notes, drafts, screenshots, old outputs, and half-finished files. The workflow should pick that context up instead of making you explain it again.
Reply text: yes. The half-finished files usually carry more useful context than another prompt.
